name: pathfinder
tagline: Orchestrator skill that maps a codebase into feature flowcharts, finds duplicated logic, and proposes a unified architecture plus handoff pr
verdict: Clean, read-only orchestration skill: no network calls, no credential access, no destructive file ops, writes only new dated report files. Steps are concrete, evidence-gated, and include failure-mode handling. Minor gaps: no explicit negative-trigger guidance, no versioning signal, and re-running the same day could collide on the date-stamped output folder.

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do NOT use when' guidance is given, e.g. for small/single-feature repos or when implementation (not analysis) is wanted; a user could invoke it in cases where the heavyweight multi-phase

Output folder is date-stamped only ('PATHFINDER-<YYYY-MM-DD>/', line 26) with no guidance for handling a second run on the same day; a re-run could silently overwrite or collide with a prior run's art
Skill instructs deploying multiple subagents and running grep/file reads but the frontmatter declares no 'allowed-tools' or tools field to scope this access explicitly.
